_about: This is the single source of truth (SSOT) for the myPKA scaffold version and for the framework/user-state seam. The scaffold version lives HERE, authoritatively. VERSION and .scaffold-version are kept for back-compat with older tooling, but they mirror this file; if they ever disagree, manifest.json wins. The framework_paths and user_state_paths blocks are machine-readable: the updater (scripts/update-scaffold.py) reads them to decide which files it MAY overwrite (framework, ours, upgradable) and which it must NEVER touch (user state, yours, sacred). Markdown is canonical; this JSON is the control plane that lets the folder update itself safely.

version_files.note: VERSION and .scaffold-version are legacy plain-text mirrors kept for back-compat. They must equal scaffold_version above. manifest.json is authoritative.

framework_paths._about: Allow-list (globs) of framework-owned paths the updater MAY overwrite. This is the engine: ours, shipped with the scaffold, upgradable. If a path is not on this list, the updater will not write it. A locally-modified file on this list is backed up before it is overwritten (never a silent overwrite of a member edit).

user_state_paths._about: Explicitly sacred. The updater NEVER writes any path under these globs, even if a future bug or a malformed target manifest claims otherwise. This is the user's own knowledge, their team's durable memory, their installed Expansion state, their secrets, and their derived databases. These survive every update by LOCATION, not by merge logic (the Hermes-Agent lesson: separate the engine directory from the state directory).

expansions._about: Each installed Expansion is versioned on its OWN expansion.yaml SemVer, NOT on the scaffold version. The scaffold updater treats all of Expansions/*/ as user-state (it never overwrites Expansion code). It only READS these declared versions so it can DETECT a behind Expansion (for example a cockpit older than the latest released cockpit) and DEFER to that Expansion's own updater. The scaffold updater never updates Expansion code itself. As of 5.0.0 the only bundled Expansion is the myPKA Cockpit (included and free). Agent packs (the Designer Pack, the App Developer Pack, and more) are part of the myICOR membership and install from the Expansion Packs page in the myICOR app; installed packs may register here so the updater can detect behind versions.

items.name: myPKA Cockpit
items.note: Runtime Expansion, bundled and free. Has source code, an npm build, and its own SQLite migration lifecycle. The scaffold updater must NOT swap cockpit code. If the cockpit is behind its latest release, the scaffold updater only prints a notice and points the member at the cockpit updater.

update_check._about: Configuration for the boot-time version check (scripts/check-version.py). ANNOUNCED-ON by default per Tom's decision. The boot-time call is the ONLY network reach in the update core. It SENDS NO USER DATA: it fetches a single remote version string over HTTPS, read-only, and fails silent when offline. It reports back to the member, in one line, if a newer scaffold version exists. It never auto-applies anything.
update_check.disclosure: On boot, myPKA fetches one version string from the official repo to tell you if an update exists. That request sends no data about you or your vault. It is read-only and fails silently if you are offline. Set update_check.enabled to false to turn it off.

changes._about: Per-path change record for THIS release (4.1.1 -> 5.0.0), so the updater can print a plain-English plan. change_type is one of: added | changed | moved. HEADLINE of 5.0.0: the scaffold returns to the six core specialists (Larry, Nolan, Pax, Penn, Mack, Silas) PLUS the bundled myPKA Cockpit, free. The Designer Pack (Iris, Charta, Pixel) and the App Developer Pack (Felix, Vex, Vera) are no longer bundled in the download; they are Expansion Packs available with the myICOR membership on the Expansion Packs page in the myICOR app. Neither the scaffold nor the Cockpit went paid. IMPORTANT for existing bundled installs: Expansions/*/ is user-state, so the updater never removes an installed pack; your packs keep working and update via their own Expansion update paths. Fresh downloads simply no longer include them. Also fixed: update_check.remote_version_url pointed at a non-existent repo (myICOR/mypka-scaffold); it now points at myICOR/myPKA (issue #15).

paths.note: scaffold_version 4.1.1 -> 5.0.0; agent-pack items removed from expansions.items (cockpit stays); remote_version_url fixed to myICOR/myPKA (issue #15).
Prepended the [5.0.0] entry (agent packs unbundled; Cockpit stays bundled and free).
Rewritten for the basic scaffold: six core specialists plus the bundled Cockpit; agent packs via myICOR membership.
Team table back to the six core specialists; Expansion-install triggers unchanged (mechanism stays).
First-run bootstrap builds the bundled Cockpit; no bundled agent-pack install steps.
Section 8-ter reworked: Cockpit build flow stays; agent-pack verification removed.
Added the 4.x -> 5.0.0 note (no action needed for existing folders).

control_folder._about: The .mypka/ hidden control folder is user-state (listed under user_state_paths). The updater creates it on first run. It holds backups/<timestamp>/ (pre-overwrite copies of any locally-modified framework file), an install/update log, and a copy of the active manifest. It is never overwritten by an update.
